steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content: We have $$\angle A = \angle P = 58°$$. To prove triangle similarity with minimal additional information, we use the AA criterion—we need just one more pair of equal angles.

steps.title: Evaluate choice C
steps.content:
Given $$\angle B = 34°$$ and $$\angle R = 88°$$:

In triangle ABC: $$\angle C = 180° - 58° - 34° = 88°$$
In triangle PQR: $$\angle Q = 180° - 58° - 88° = 34°$$
Result:
  • $$\angle A = \angle P = 58°$$
  • $$\angle B = \angle Q = 34°$$
  • $$\angle C = \angle R = 88°$$
All three angles match across the correspondence A ↔ P, B ↔ Q, C ↔ R. That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Verify other choices fail
steps.content: Choice D creates angles 58°, 58°, 64° in ABC but 58°, 34°, 88° in PQR—no correspondence exists. Choices A and B lack the proportionality $$\frac{AB}{AC} = \frac{PQ}{PR}$$ that SAS similarity requires.
